description_long: This object is a historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden carriage, featuring a long, cylindrical black metal barrel with a slightly flared muzzle. The barrel is secured within a robust, dark brown wooden frame that sits on four solid wooden wheels, each reinforced with metal hardware. The carriage includes metal rings on the sides, likely for maneuvering or securing the cannon. The wood appears aged and textured, with visible grain and bolts, while the metal barrel has a matte, slightly worn finish. The overall construction is sturdy, with the cannon barrel resting in a cradle cut into the carriage. This object evokes the look of artillery used in historical urban fortifications or ceremonial displays.
description: A historical black metal cannon mounted on a dark wooden wheeled carriage, featuring metal rings and reinforced bolts.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the black metal barrel and the detailed wooden carriage with wheels and metal ring.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular muzzle of the barrel and the symmetrical arrangement of the wooden carriage and wheels.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first side but with subtle differences in wood grain and hardware visibility.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back end of the barrel and the structure of the carriage, including the rear wheels and bolts.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long cylindrical barrel with no visible ribbing or venting
